nodes.name: gamboa_segmentation
nodes.description: Segments a signal into isoelectric and non-isoelectric regions based on the Gamboa (2008) method. This algorithm identifies segments by analyzing the standard deviation of the signal within a moving window.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The sampling frequency of the signal in Hz.

inputs.name: tol
inputs.constraints: The tolerance for the segmentation, typically a small value like 0.001.

outputs.name: segments
outputs.constraints: An array of indices indicating the start and end points of the identified segments.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, tol: float) -> np.ndarray
nodes.conceptual_summary: local_variance_partitioner. Partitions an ordered 1D numerical sequence into contiguous subsequences. This is achieved by moving a sliding window across the sequence and calculating the standard deviation within it. Regions where this local standard deviation is below a specified tolerance are classified differently from regions where it is above, producing a set of boundaries that delineate these contiguous segments of low and high variance.. Applications: In finance, to segment a time-series of asset prices into periods of high volatility (risk) and low volatility (stability)., In materials science, to analyze sensor data from a manufacturing process to detect anomalies, where a change in output variance could indicate a flaw or process deviation., In genomics, to identify conserved (low-variance) versus hypervariable (high-variance) regions within a multiple sequence alignment, which can imply structural or functional importance.

nodes.name: convert_time_windows_to_samples
nodes.description: Converts time durations in milliseconds to the equivalent number of samples based on the sampling rate.

outputs.name: window_100ms
outputs.constraints: Number of samples in a 100ms window.

outputs.name: window_300ms
outputs.constraints: Number of samples in a 300ms window.

nodes.name: estimate_signal_amplitude_range
nodes.description: Calculates the effective amplitude range of the signal by creating a histogram and identifying the boundaries that exclude the top and bottom 1% of the signal distribution, effectively ignoring outliers.

outputs.name: amplitude_thresholds
outputs.constraints: A tuple containing the lower (v0) and upper (v1) amplitude boundaries.

nodes.name: normalize_signal
nodes.description: Normalizes the input signal to a [-1, 1] range based on the maximum absolute value of the estimated amplitude thresholds.

inputs.name: amplitude_thresholds
inputs.constraints: The lower and upper amplitude boundaries.

outputs.name: normalized_signal
outputs.constraints: Signal with values scaled to the [-1, 1] range.

nodes.name: find_concave_candidate_peaks
nodes.description: Identifies candidate R-peaks by finding points of high concavity in the normalized signal. This is achieved by locating the peaks of the inverted second derivative of the signal that exceed a given tolerance.

inputs.name: tolerance
inputs.constraints: The minimum concavity magnitude to be considered a candidate peak.

outputs.name: candidate_peak_indices
outputs.constraints: An array of indices corresponding to potential R-peaks.

nodes.name: filter_and_refine_peaks
nodes.description: Filters the candidate peaks by enforcing a minimum time separation between them (a refractory period of 300ms). For each valid candidate, it refines the peak location by finding the absolute maximum in the original signal within a 100ms window following the candidate index.

inputs.name: refractory_period_samples
inputs.constraints: The minimum number of samples between consecutive R-peaks (from 300ms window).

inputs.name: search_window_samples
inputs.constraints: The number of samples in the forward search window for refining the peak (from 100ms window).

outputs.name: filtered_rpeaks
outputs.constraints: A list of refined R-peak indices.

nodes.type_signature: (candidate_peak_indices: np.ndarray, original_signal: np.ndarray, refractory_period_samples: int, search_window_samples: int) -> list[int]

nodes.name: finalize_rpeaks
nodes.description: Ensures the final list of R-peak indices is unique and sorted in chronological order, then converts it to an integer numpy array.

inputs.name: filtered_rpeaks
inputs.constraints: The list of R-peak indices, potentially with duplicates or out of order.

outputs.name: rpeaks
outputs.constraints: A sorted array of unique R-peak indices.
